iwn.ALSO_SEE.description: Other words that are similar or similar interest

iwn.HOLO_MEMBER_COLLECTION.description: acts as a "has-a" or "part-of" relation, linking a part (meronym) to the whole entity (holonym), specifically where the part is an individual member of that group (e.g., ship -> fleet)

iwn.TROPONYMY.description: the specific semantic relation that links verbs, acting as a "manner" relation, Ex. Whisper is a troponym of talk (it is a way of talking).March is a troponym of walk. Nibble or gobble are troponyms of eat.

iwn.MERO_COMPONENT_OBJECT.description: component-meronym relationship, where the meronym (part) is a physical, distinguishable component of a holonym (whole/object)., such as wheel being a MERO_COMPONENT_OBJECT of car

iwn.HOLO_PLACE_AREA.description: to represent the "holonym" of a place or area. It defines a "part-of" or "member-of" relationship where a particular place, location, or feature is a constituent part of a larger area.Ex काबा is in मक्का

iwn.ONTOLOGY.description: Defines ontology of the word, details for the same row will provide sort order in parenthesis of these, Ranking lowest to highest, The wordList provides description in Hindi followed by english in paranthesis,

iwn.MERO_POSITION_AREA.description: suggests the component is a specific, locational part of a larger area or object. like "finger" is a meronym of "hand"

iwn.MERO_PLACE_AREA.description: defines a "part-of" relationship (meronym/holonym) where the place (meronym) is geographically located within a broader location or area (holonym), such as a city within a state

iwn.ABILITY_VERB.description: refers to a category of verbs that describe the capacity, skill, or opportunity to perform an action

iwn.MERO_PORTION_MASS.description: a relationship where a particular concept is a "portion" or a subset of a "mass" or substance.

iwn.FUNCTION_VERB.description: refers to a refers to a lexico-semantic relation that links a noun (usually an object or artifact) to the verb representing its typical action or function.

iwn.HOLO_STUFF_OBJECT.description: is a specific relation type used in the context of meronymy (part-whole relationships) to represent that a particular substance ("stuff") is a constituent component of a whole object

iwn.HOLO_PORTION_MASS.description: represent the relationship between a whole entity and a mass of material that constitutes a portion of it. Ex If considering "milk" and "glass," "milk" might be considered a portion mass of "glass" (depending on how the synsets are structured), where "glass" is the holonym.

Roots.description: Etymological roots of the word, comma seperted in the order of application or usage

iwn.MERO_MEMBER_COLLECTION.description: relation connects a specific entity (member) to a group or collection that it belongs to (the holonym) example tree (member) -> forest (collection), professor (member) -> faculty (collection)

iwn.MODIFIES_NOUN.description: used in lexical-semantic networks to relate a modifier concept to the concept it modifies. Establishes a conceptual dependency, linking concepts like {healthy} to {life} to represent, for example, a healthy life

iwn.CAUSATIVE.description: a semantic relation between verbs where one verb causes or brings about the state or action of another (e.g., give is a causative of have)

iwn.MERO_STUFF_OBJECT.description: link materials to the items made from them (e.g., "wood" is the MERO_STUFF_OBJECT of "table").

iwn.HYPERNYMY.description: a semantic, hierarchical "is-a" relationship where a hypernym (broader term) includes the meaning of a hyponym (specific term). It organizes nouns and verbs into a tree structure, often used to evaluate NLP models or for automated text classification

iwn.CAPABILITY_VERB.description: indicates that one verb implies or requires the ability to perform another, linking a verb that denotes a "capability" to its corresponding activity.

iwn.MODIFIES_VERB.description: to specify that an adverb (or adjective acting as an adverb) describes the manner, time, or intensity of a particular action verb. Usually more than one of those seperated by comma
